completed_tasks.name: Li coefficients lambda_n computed exactly (FFT-Taylor) for n=1..200
Decomposition: lambda_n = lambda_n^Gamma + lambda_n^zeta via smooth_gamma + log_sz
Gamma part: negative n=1..7, positive n>=8, grows like (n/2)*ln(n)
Zeta part: oscillates, goes NEGATIVE at n=156-186 (min -1.026 at n=172)
Total always positive — Gamma dominates for all n
Stieltjes constant expansion validated against FFT
M-function connection: Li coefficients average M-function over circle
Tightest constraint: lambda_1 = 0.023 (4.2% margin, n=1 is hardest)
Correction ratio: peaks at 1.010 (n=171), gamma floor breaks for n>=156

remaining_tasks.name: SESSION 18: Prove asymptotic bound |lambda_n^zeta| = O(sqrt(n*log n))
Determine N_0 such that lambda_n^Gamma > |lambda_n^zeta| for all n >= N_0
Express lambda_n^zeta growth rate via zero density theorems
Test truncated Euler product approach (Avenue 4 from Grok)
Connes prolate operator connection (Avenue 5 from Grok)

decisions.decision: Li coefficient decomposition: Gamma part from smooth_gamma(s), zeta part from log((s-1)*zeta(s))
decisions.rationale: Natural split: Gamma/pi factors vs prime distribution. Gamma part is explicitly computable, zeta part encodes arithmetic.

decisions.decision: lambda_n^zeta CAN be negative (n=156-186), disproving initial hypothesis
decisions.rationale: Higher precision (200dp) computation revealed oscillation with growing amplitude. The 'gamma floor' argument (d_1=gamma dominates) fails for large n.

decisions.decision: Total lambda_n always positive because Gamma part grows like n*log(n), dominating bounded zeta oscillation
decisions.rationale: Gamma ~ (n/2)ln(n), zeta ~ O(sqrt(n*log n)) expected from equidistribution of zero phases. At n=172 where zeta=-1.03, Gamma=+268.

decisions.decision: Oscillation of lambda_n^zeta has period ~89, determined by first zero gamma_1=14.13
decisions.rationale: Phase angle alpha_rho = arg(1-1/rho) for rho on critical line. alpha_1 = 2/gamma_1 asymptotically. Period = 2*pi/alpha_1 = 88.8.

next_action: Session 18: (1) Prove asymptotic |lambda_n^zeta| = O(sqrt(n*log n)) using zero density, (2) Determine threshold N_0 for Gamma dominance, (3) Avenue 4: truncated Euler product in M-function, (4) Consider whether the decomposition reduces RH to a finite computation.
context_notes: Session 17 explored Li's criterion (RH iff lambda_n > 0 for all n). Key scripts: li_criterion.py (basic computation), li_prime_anatomy.py (Stieltjes connection), li_extended.py (n=1..100), li_highorder.py (n=1..200, 200dp), li_M_connection.py (M-function link). Used FFT-based Taylor coefficients (Cauchy integral) since mpmath.taylor/diff failed at s=1 zeta pole. Decomposition into smooth_gamma (Gamma/pi part) and log_sz (prime part) is the central tool. Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe.
